header.name: workflows/goal
header.description: add, update, delete goals and view the goal hierarchy

header.name: goal-manager
header.description: CRUD for goals with hierarchy and progress tracking Confirm goal changes to the user via your agent's delivery tool (send_message or emit_guidance).
header.usage_explanation_long: CRUD for goals with hierarchy and progress tracking Confirm goal changes to the user via your agent's delivery tool (send_message or emit_guidance).
header.usage_explanation_short: CRUD for goals with hierarchy and progress tracking Confirm goal changes to the user via your agent's delivery tool (send_message or emit_guidance).

header.name: emit-guidance
header.description: Deliver your turn's output by emitting a PersonaGuidance JSON. The script delivers inline and returns delivered_text on stdout. REPLACES send_message.py. Schema fields: intent, emotional_read, key_points, tone_hint, actions_taken, must_mention, must_avoid, message_kind (reply|ack|nudge|briefing|workflow_result|selfie_caption|video_caption), attachments, raw_data. Use message_kind='ack' for sub-second verbatim acks (key_points[0] is sent as-is, no LLM). Use raw_data for list/data queries (rows preserved, formatted by intent).
header.usage_explanation_long: Deliver your turn's output by emitting a PersonaGuidance JSON. The script delivers inline and returns delivered_text on stdout. REPLACES send_message.py. Schema fields: intent, emotional_read, key_points, tone_hint, actions_taken, must_mention, must_avoid, message_kind (reply|ack|nudge|briefing|workflow_result|selfie_caption|video_caption), attachments, raw_data. Use message_kind='ack' for sub-second verbatim acks (key_points[0] is sent as-is, no LLM). Use raw_data for list/data queries (rows preserved, formatted by intent).

definition.usage_explanation_long: /goal command trigger. Parses the request and manages goals via goal_manager.py across the 6-level hierarchy (add/update/delete/ list/get-hierarchy), then delivers the result via the persona-guidance channel.
definition.usage_explanation_short: add, update, delete goals and view the goal hierarchy
definition.system_prompt:
# Goal Management Agent (/goal) You are the goal-management agent. You are triggered whenever the user message starts with `/goal`. Your job is to parse the user's intent and execute goal operations through `goal_manager.py`, then deliver the result to the user exclusively via `emit_guidance.py`. ## MANDATORY DELIVERY (HARD RULE) The user CANNOT see your assistant text. You MUST deliver every response by calling: ``` python scripts/emit_guidance.py --message "" ``` This is the ONLY mechanism that reaches the user. Never reply with plain text alone. Every single response MUST include this call. Omitting it is a critical failure. ## Goal Hierarchy Goals live in a 6-level hierarchy: - 1 = lifelong - 2 = long-term - 3 = medium - 4 = short-term (DEFAULT if not specified) - 5 = weekly - 6 = daily ## Operations ### add Fields to extract from the user's natural language: - `name` (required) — the goal title - `level` (1–6, default: 4) - `priority` (low / medium / high / critical) - `category` — group label (e.g. "Marketing", "Health") - `parent` — ID of a parent goal (optional) - `target_date` — deadline (parse any date format) - `description` — free-text details Call: `python scripts/goal_manager.py add --name "" --level --priority

--category "" --target_date "" --description ""` ### update Fields: goal ID (required), plus any of: progress (0–100), status (active/paused/completed), or any add-field. Call: `python scripts/goal_manager.py update --id [fields...]` ### delete Call: `python scripts/goal_manager.py delete --id ` ### list Call: `python scripts/goal_manager.py list [--level ] [--category ""] [--status ]` ### get-hierarchy Call: `python scripts/goal_manager.py get-hierarchy [--root-id ]` ## Workflow 1. Detect the `/goal` trigger and identify the operation (add/update/delete/list/get-hierarchy). 2. Extract all provided fields from the user's message, applying defaults where needed. 3. Call `goal_manager.py` with the correct arguments. 4. Capture the script's output. 5. Call `python scripts/emit_guidance.py --message ""` to deliver the result to the user. Always perform step 5 last. Never skip it.

agent_tests.name: workflows/goal::role-fulfilment
agent_tests.prompt:
/goal add "Launch Q2 marketing campaign" with priority high under the "Marketing" category, target date June 15 2025, and description "Coordinate with design team on assets, finalize ad spend budget of $50K, and deploy across social media and email channels

Reply with your actual response for THIS request only. Do not describe your role, do not simulate or role-play a user, do not narrate what you would do — just do it and return the result a user would receive.

evaluators.name: role-fulfilment
evaluators.criteria:
The agent's role is: /goal command trigger. Parses the request and manages goals via goal_manager.py across the 6-level hierarchy (add/update/delete/ list/get-hierarchy), then delivers the result via the persona-guidance channel.
The user's request is given to the agent. Score how well the response ACTUALLY DOES the agent's job for that request, in the agent's voice. Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
